mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is above its 12-month rolling median, it signals capacity constraints that lead to higher input costs and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for this signal is below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or the mean 20-day return in the top 30% INDPRO decile is not negative at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% reflects an overheated labor market that forces wage inflation and erodes corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0, or the average return does not differ from zero at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts and retail sales both rise above their respective 3-month moving averages, it indicates synchronized consumer and residential demand strength that boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average 20-day return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation is negative (negative real rates), equities benefit from low discount rates and a search for yield, producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return during negative real rate episodes is not above the mean during positive real rate episodes (one-sided test p > 0.05), or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000 (indicating robust job creation), it signals rising aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day return following months with PAYEMS > 150000 is not greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and above-median housing starts jointly captures accommodative monetary policy transmitting into real estate strength, which lifts equity prices via wealth effects and construction employment over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC of the binary interaction signal is below 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows, or the average 5-day return when both conditions hold is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) on a year-over-year basis reflects strengthening consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in all regimes.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not positively correlated with the year-over-year change in RSAFS (Pearson r < 0.10 over 10-year rolling period), or the top tercile of RSAFS changes does not produce mean returns above the bottom tercile.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion regime, an increase in industrial production accompanied by a very low unemployment rate signals overheating and imminent profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (low unemployment, high capacity utilization)
failure_condition: Mean 20-day return during late-cycle episodes (UNRATE < 4.5% and INDPRO > its 80th percentile) is not negative, or the signal fails to distinguish returns from early-cycle episodes (unequal means t-test p > 0.05).
